wine_restaurants.name: Piazza Duomo
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Modern Piedmontese haute cuisine by Enrico Crippa; vegetable-forward tasting menus engineered around Langhe terroir with a deep Nebbiolo-led cellar
wine_restaurants.description: Enrico Crippa's three-Michelin-starred dining room above Piazza Risorgimento in Alba, opened 2005 under the Ceretto family. Vegetable-driven tasting menus with a Barolo and Barbaresco-anchored cellar.

wine_restaurants.name: All'Enoteca
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Refined Piedmontese and Roero cooking by Davide Palluda; menus built around Roero Arneis and Nebbiolo with a regional cellar
wine_restaurants.description: Davide Palluda's Michelin-starred restaurant above the Enoteca Regionale del Roero in Canale, open since 1995 and starred since 2000. Roero-centred wine list with Arneis whites and Nebbiolo reds.

wine_restaurants.name: La Ciau del Tornavento
wine_restaurants.address: Piazza Leopoldo Baracco 7, 12050 Treiso CN, Italy
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Piedmontese and contemporary Italian cooking in the Barbaresco hills; one of Italy's most complete wine lists across a tuff-carved cellar
wine_restaurants.description: Michelin-starred restaurant in a 1931 building above the Barbaresco vineyards in Treiso. The tuff-hill cellar holds about 60,000 bottles from 450 winemakers and roughly 1,800 labels.

wine_restaurants.name: Guido Ristorante
wine_restaurants.address: Via Alba 15, 12050 Serralunga d'Alba CN, Italy
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Classic Langhe cooking by the Alciati family inside the Fontanafredda estate; cellar built on Barolo and the wider Fontanafredda range
wine_restaurants.description: Michelin-starred restaurant in the royal Fontanafredda estate at Serralunga d'Alba, run by the Alciati family. Memory-driven Piedmontese cooking with a Barolo-anchored cellar drawn from the estate.

wine_restaurants.name: Locanda del Pilone
wine_restaurants.address: Frazione Madonna di Como 34, 12051 Alba CN, Italy
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Piedmontese and Mediterranean cooking by chef Federico Gallo; a 1,400-label list strong in Barolo, Barbaresco and French crus
wine_restaurants.description: Michelin-starred restaurant and resort on the Madonna di Como hill above Alba, with 360-degree vineyard views. Chef Federico Gallo cooks the Langhe; the list runs to more than 1,400 labels.

wine_restaurants.name: Ristorante Bovio
wine_restaurants.address: Via Alba 17/Bis, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Traditional Langhe cooking above the family vineyard; a Barolo-led list anchored on the Bovio estate wines and La Morra producers
wine_restaurants.description: Family restaurant in La Morra perched above the Bovio vineyard, run by Alessandra Bovio. Classic Piedmontese plates and a terrace looking across the Barolo cru toward the Alps; the list leans on local Barolo.

wine_restaurants.name: Vincafe
wine_restaurants.address: Via Vittorio Emanuele 12, 12051 Alba CN, Italy
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Piedmontese wine-bar dining in central Alba; tajarin and truffle plates paired from a deep Langhe by-the-glass and bottle list
wine_restaurants.description: Vineria and restaurant on Alba's main pedestrian street, pairing Piedmontese plates such as tagliolino with white truffle to a broad Langhe wine list. Casual counter and table service in central Alba.

wine_restaurants.name: Osteria dell'Enoteca
wine_restaurants.cuisine: Roero osteria cooking on the first floor of the Enoteca Regionale del Roero; informal Palluda plates with a Roero Arneis and Nebbiolo list
wine_restaurants.description: Davide Palluda's informal osteria opened 2013 below his starred All'Enoteca, inside the Enoteca Regionale del Roero in Canale. Roero classics on the plate with the consortium's Arneis and Nebbiolo wines.
